title: salix frigens
intro: Salix fragilis, also known as the crack willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Salix fragilis include reducing fever, alleviating pain and swelling, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious conditions such as arthritis and eczema.
